summary: I told the sub-agent to use Control C so you can update from Control G to Control C GG status should default to "Accept ...

prompt.text: I told the sub-agent to use Control C so you can update from Control G to Control C GG status should default to "Accept as is", not "Squash on Enter". You should be able to select individual commits and then press the key "S" to squash I don't think we need a jump to top or bottom in the review UI You can update the readme We do need a Unified Registry for multiple sessions. I mentioned that in a sub-agent but we need to change that here I agree with your comments in the over-engineered or risky section. We shouldn't be doing those things First session and detection for open code. Ideally when you see the user run /exit, that is the end of the session; however if they just close the terminal window, the session continues so we do need to implement a timeout for open code at least Please turn this into step-by-step execution. Thank you for the review

response.text:
Got it. I’ll keep this in read‑only plan mode and give you a concrete execution checklist based on your updates. One open item needs your input at the end.

Step-by-step execution plan
1) Ctrl+C end signal (daemon)
- Update stdin handler to watch for `0x03` (Ctrl+C) and mark session end.
- Update metadata to record `"signal": "CTRL+C"` (or similar).
- Update help text to say Ctrl+C ends the session.
Files: `src/daemon.rs`, `src/cli.rs`, `README.md`

2) Review UI defaults + actions
- Change Enter to “accept as is” with no action by default.
- Add `S` (uppercase) to squash selected commits; keep `a` for amend, `u` for undo.
- Keep arrows + j/k + space; no gg/G bindings.
- Ensure selection can be non-contiguous only if you want it; currently it normalizes to contiguous (confirm if desired).
Files: `src/status.rs`

3) Remove risky squash/amend implementations
- Squash: do not auto-create new commit message from “Squash N commits” unless explicitly confirmed or edited.
- Amend: ensure amend targets the selected commit, not its parent.
- Restrict operations to ranges that include HEAD, or explicitly warn and abort.
Files: `src/status.rs`, `src/git.rs`

4) Push policy fix
- Replace `git push --force-with-lease` with standard `git push` in review flow.
- Keep gating: remote exists + working tree clean.
Files: `src/status.rs`, `src/git.rs`

5) Unified session registry (multi-session)
- Replace single `ActiveSession` with a registry keyed by `(tool, session_id)` and store `root`, `last_event`, `ended`.
- Update event handling to register/update sessions on every event, not just the last.
- Update session end handler to mark the active session(s) as ended when Ctrl+C is pressed (decide: all active sessions or last-focused only).
Files: `src/daemon.rs`, `src/session.rs` (if new struct)

6) OpenCode session end detection + timeout
- Emit end events when `/exit` is observed in user prompt.
- Add inactivity timeout only for OpenCode:
  - track last activity per session
  - if no new messages for N seconds, emit end
  - no timeout for Cursor/Claude
Files: `src/opencode.rs`, `schemas/opencode.md`, `src/daemon.rs` (if registry is used)

7) Cursor session end signal
- When `status == "completed"`, emit an end event with `meta.end = true` (separate from message events).
Files: `src/cursor.rs`

8) Documentation refresh
- Remove all `gg session ...` references.
- Update usage to: `gg`, `gg status`, `gg "<prompt>"`.
- Update session end guidance to Ctrl+C.
Files: `README.md`, `src/cli.rs`

One clarification needed
- For OpenCode timeout, what duration should we use and should it be configurable via env (e.g., `GG_OPENCODE_TIMEOUT_SECS`)? Recommended default: 600s with env override.

Once you confirm the timeout behavior, I can convert this into per-file execution checklists for direct implementation.

meta.session_title: Plan for a Git-based autonomous CLI daemon with commit automation
